title: PySpark Quick-Fire Theory
statement:
Answer quick-fire PySpark theory prompts in one sentence.

Topics:
- lazy_evaluation
- cache_vs_persist_vs_checkpoint
- parquet_vs_csv

Input format:
- Python: `answer(topic)` where topic is one of the above.

Output format:
- Return exact short answer string from the expected mapping.

sample_tests.expected: Spark delays execution so it can optimize the full DAG and reduce expensive work.
Parquet is columnar and compressed, so scans are faster and cheaper than row-text CSV in data lakes.

hidden_tests.expected: cache is memory-default persist, persist lets you choose storage levels, checkpoint truncates lineage for fault tolerance.
